# OSSICRO Citation Inventory

**GENERATED** from the single-source table `engine/ossicro/citations.py` — regenerate with `python -m ossicro.citations > ../docs/regulatory/CITATION-INVENTORY.md` (from `engine/`). Do not edit this file by hand; edit the table.

**Human-verification rule (HITL).** A row flips from `PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION` to `HUMAN-VERIFIED` ONLY when a qualified human verifies the pinpoint against eCFR and records their name and date in the table in `citations.py`. AI/persona review is not a substitute. While any row a document uses is pending, the rendered document carries the pending-footer line.

| Key | Pinpoint | Status | Verified by | Verified date | Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `charging.consent_disclosure` | 21 CFR 312.8(d) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Cost-recovery: charging limited to direct costs; the consent cost statement cross-cites it. |
| `charging.expanded_access` | 21 CFR 312.8 | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Charging for investigational drugs under an IND (submission.cost_recovery_statement; first appearance of 312.8 in the repo — P4 routes the LOA-request charging line through this row). |
| `clock.ind_annual_report` | 21 CFR 312.33 | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Annual report within 60 CALENDAR days of each anniversary of the IND-effective date (effective = receipt + 30 per 312.40(b)(1); Feb-29 effective dates anniversary on Feb 28). |
| `clock.ind_effective_30_day` | 21 CFR 312.40(b)(1) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | 30 CALENDAR days after FDA receipt: IND effective absent hold or earlier notification. |
| `clock.irb_emergency_notification` | 21 CFR 56.104(c) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | 5 WORKING days: IRB notification after emergency use. Anchor: the emergency USE, i.e. the FIRST-TREATMENT date — never the FDA authorization date (M1). |
| `clock.safety_report_15` | 21 CFR 312.32(c)(1) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | 15 CALENDAR days: IND safety report of a serious and unexpected suspected adverse reaction, per event. |
| `clock.safety_report_7` | 21 CFR 312.32(c)(2) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | 7 CALENDAR days: fatal/life-threatening unexpected suspected adverse reaction, from initial receipt, per event. |
| `clock.written_3926` | 21 CFR 312.310(d)(2) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | 15 WORKING days: written 3926 after FDA emergency (telephone) authorization. Anchor: the AUTHORIZATION date. |
| `consent.element_costs` | 21 CFR 50.25(b)(3) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Additional-costs consent element (consent.cost_statement; pairs with the 312.8(d) cost-recovery disclosure). |
| `consent.element_injury_compensation` | 21 CFR 50.25(a)(6) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Consent element 6: whether compensation and whether medical treatments are available if injury occurs (consent.injury_compensation_statement; P6 renders it). |
| `consent.element_new_findings` | 21 CFR 50.25(b)(5) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Additional consent element: significant new findings that may affect willingness to continue (the EA ICF's new-information paragraph; P6 renders it). |
| `consent.element_stopping` | 21 CFR 50.25(b)(4) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Additional consent element: consequences of a decision to withdraw and procedures for orderly termination (the EA ICF's stopping-treatment paragraph; P6 renders it). |
| `consent.emergency_exception` | 21 CFR 50.23 | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Exception from the general consent requirements (emergency use); the documented-exception arm of consent.timing_attestation. |
| `consent.general_requirements` | 21 CFR 50.20 | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | General requirements for informed consent — the attestation frame for consent.timing_attestation. |
| `ea.consent.treatment_framing` | FDA Guidance: Individual Patient Expanded Access Applications - Form FDA 3926 (treatment-use consent framing) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | The EA ICF opens with treatment-use framing (treatment, not a research study) per FDA's individual-patient expanded-access guidance, resolving the therapeutic misconception in both directions. Guidance-based, not a CFR pinpoint: the human verifier confirms the framing against the current guidance edition. |
| `ea.end_of_treatment_summary` | 21 CFR 312.310(c)(2) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | Written summary of the expanded-access use, including adverse effects, at the conclusion of treatment (treatment.conclusion_date arms it; P7 renders the obligations row). |
| `ea.treatment_plan.cmc` | 21 CFR 312.305(b)(2)(vi) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | M8 remap: was (b)(2)(v). Chemistry, manufacturing, and controls. |
| `ea.treatment_plan.dosing` | 21 CFR 312.305(b)(2)(iv) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | M8 remap: was (b)(2)(iii). Method of administration, dose, duration; treatment-plan summary and composed dosing_plan. |
| `ea.treatment_plan.facility` | 21 CFR 312.305(b)(2)(v) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | M8 remap: was (b)(2)(iv). Facility description / site.name. |
| `ea.treatment_plan.monitoring` | 21 CFR 312.305(b)(2)(viii) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | M8 remap: was (b)(2)(vii). JUDGMENT CALL for the human verifier: monitoring is mapped to (viii) — the treatment plan's clinical monitoring to evaluate drug effects and minimize risk — NOT to the (iv) administration-adjacent procedures. Confirm against eCFR. |
| `ea.treatment_plan.patient_description` | 21 CFR 312.305(b)(2)(iii) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | M8 remap: was (b)(2)(ii). Patient description / diagnosis / prior therapies / coded identifier. |
| `ea.treatment_plan.pharmtox` | 21 CFR 312.305(b)(2)(vii) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | M8 remap: was (b)(2)(vi). Pharmacology and toxicology. |
| `ea.treatment_plan.rationale` | 21 CFR 312.305(b)(2)(ii) | PENDING-HUMAN-VERIFICATION | — | — | M8 remap: was (b)(2)(i). Rationale for the intended treatment use. |
